Across the square, Chantelle bumps into Denise but is left confused when she mentions a fight Gray was involved in, and Linda and Mick take a trip down memory lane(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: A paranoid Jac feels she is the centre of hospital gossip when word spreads Emma is now living with Jonny. Elliot's concern for her grows, but feels unable to tell her his secret. Lofty resolves to put his new family first, but even the basics of parenting stir up trouble between him, Helen and Dom. Sacha struggles with his feelings for Essie, which seem doomed to go unrequited. Guest starring Vicky Entwistle (Coronation Street)(n)

programme.title: Gold Digger
programme.desc: Drama, starring Julia Ormond as a wealthy middle-aged woman who begins a relationship with a man nearly 20 years younger than she is just as her divorce is finalised. When he is introduced to her three adult children, suspicions are raised about his motivations. The action cuts between the early days of their relationship and the wedding day, which seems fated to end in disaster. With Ben Barnes and Jemima Rooper(n)

programme.title: Love and Hate Crime
programme.desc: Two murders in Houston, Texas, that left baffled police thinking there was an Iranian hit squad at work. Ali Isran, who was convicted of the crimes, tells his story from death row, and the film follows the extraordinary twists and turns of the case(n)

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the fifth episode, Jared and Sophie, a couple from Essex, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon(n)

programme.title: Earth from Space
programme.desc: Cameras in space reveal the stories behind Earth's most remarkable patterns and shapes, including fresh water lagoons in Brazil, mysterious light spots in the Australian outback, endless green canopy in the Congo rainforest, and the twists and turns of the Amazon river which provides a home for rehabilitated manatees(n)

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather, presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin(n)

A team of experts and volunteers come together to help transform an old church into a new accommodation facility for six young homeless people in Blackburn, ran by local charity Nightsafe(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: This week, the candidates are tasked to create advertising campaigns to entice people from the UK to visit Finland during the summer months. Half the team head to Finland to film an advert for television, whilst the others create a marketing campaign and a digital billboard to be displayed at London's Piccadilly circus. Next, the candidates pitch their campaigns to leading industry experts, including the Head of Tourism for Finland. Back in the boardroom, one of the candidates leaves the competition(n)

programme.title: Why Dad Killed Mum: My Family's Secret
programme.desc: A young woman searches for the answers as to why her father killed her mother, grandmother and aunt in a house fire when she was just 16 months old. Tasnim, who is now 19, has been asked to bring her opinion to the parole board as her father nears the end of his 18-year jail sentence. Her search for the truth about her mother's murder takes her back to the late 1990s as she investigates her parents' relationship, and reveals a shocking truth that takes her deep into a secret that rocked a community(n)

programme.title: Why Dad Killed Mum: My Family's Secret
programme.desc: Tasnim gains accesses court transcripts from her father's trial, which reveal reports of possessive behaviour and sexual jealousy throughout her parents' turbulent relationship. Later, a childhood friend of her Tasnim's mother also claims that her father allegedly manipulated and groomed her and other underage girls(n)

programme.title: Why Dad Killed Mum: My Family's Secret
programme.desc: In the final episode, the police release Tasnim's mother's belongings for the first time, and among the items Tasnim makes a shocking discovery which highlights the true extent of the abuse she suffered at the hands of her father. Ahead of her father's parole hearing, Tasnim looks to the future and hopes that her family can finally find justice(n)

programme.title: Inside the Supermarket
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of Sainsbury's. After a challenging Christmas, the staff go all out to maximise profits with their flower sales over the Mother's Day weekend. Plans are also made to sell a birthday cake to celebrate the company's 150th year of trading, bringing in Claire Ptak, the creator of Harry and Meghan Markle's wedding cake, for ideas. Plus, last year CEO Mike Coupe announced a possible merger with Asda. Will industry regulators give the company the go-ahead to become the biggest supermarket in the country?(n)

programme.desc: Mel and Sharon desperately try to get to Phil first. Will Sharon be able thwart Mel's plan and keep the secret that will tear her family apart hidden - or is the truth about to explode? Chantelle confronts Gray about the fight he had, but she is not convinced by his answer(n)

programme.title: Inside the Supermarket
programme.desc: As more people worry about the use of single-use plastic, cameras follow the Sainsbury's innovation team as they try to find a recyclable alternative for the plastic bag used to carry loose fruit and vegetables. But as it is launched across the country, are customers ready for such a change in shopping habits? With Easter around the corner, the supply staff decide what to stock in each store and how much, while at the clothing HQ, plans are already being drawn up for this year's Christmas fashion(n)

programme.title: The Met: Policing London
programme.desc: In Newham, east London, the Trident team is targeting one of the capital's most dangerous gangs, whose leaders groom school children to commit violent acts of crime - and DCI Jim McKee gets a lead after the kidnap and torture of a teenage boy. New Scotland Yard appeal for witnesses to come forward when a man falls victim to an attack in Trafalgar Square, and dies later from his injuries. In Ealing, response officers Naomi Pearce and Mahad Abdalla deal with a drunk-driver(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Brighton, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo ventures Down Under to explore Australia by train. He begins by riding the legendary Ghan Railway, a train almost a kilometre in length and hauled by two locomotives, which covers nearly 2,000 miles across Australia's red centre. Michael traces its route from the southern harbour of Port Augusta to Darwin on the north coast, along the way meeting the indigenous people of Alice Springs, visiting a kangaroo sanctuary and joining Australians to mark the anniversary of Anzac Day, the First Word War battle that defined their nation(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A profile of the movie actor, who died in September 2008. Newman won an Academy Award for his performance in the 1986 Martin Scorsese-directed film The Color of Money, and was nominated eight further times in his career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms(n)

programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day three from The O2 in London, featuring a singles match in the round-robin stage. South Africa's Kevin Anderson produced a dominant display to overwhelm Kei Nishikori of Japan 6-0, 6-1 in this session 12 months ago(n)

programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal chats to the couple who avoided elimination in Sunday night's dance-off, and previews the songs the couples will be dancing to this weekend in Blackpool(n)

programme.desc: Dr Ranj Singh and Dr Zoe Williams go looking for antiques in Lancashire and Greater Manchester, aided by experts Stephanie Connell and Tim Medhurst. Ranj visits a Blackpool dance hall and Zoe joins the crew of a steam train before attending an auction in Harrogate, where they hope to make a profit on their purchases(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: In the latest heat, Monica Galetti challenges three of the chefs to employ deft knife skills to spatchcock a quail before cooking it with rocket pesto in just 15 minutes. The remaining contestants prepare Marcus Wareing's beignets with chocolate sauce, before all six get the chance to redeem any earlier mistakes when they cook their own signature dish for Monica, Marcus and Gregg Wallace. The judges then decide which three have done enough to make it through to the quarter-final stage of the competition(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: The chef continues his 'journey without maps', leaving the main routes and tourist hotspots behind to embark on a personal road trip along picturesque and remote backroads. Here, he heads through France's eastern border regions of Alsace and Jura - famous for Comte cheese and home to his favourite French dish of choucroute garni. He then begins to head inland, visiting Burgundy for a memorable glass of Auxey-Duresses wine, and sampling oeuf en meurette with a difference. Following a memorable Sunday lunch near the French/Swiss border, he is inspired to recreate a traditional blueberry tart(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Sindhu Vee hosts this episode of the comedy showcase recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo, featuring stand-up performances by New York-based Edinburgh comedy award winner Alex Edelman, and at the Edinburgh Festival Fringe Comedians' Choice Award winner Lou Sanders(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories with Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: NFL This Week
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present action from the 10th round of fixtures, including Seattle Seahawks v San Francisco 49ers(n)

programme.title: Undercover with the Clerics: Iraq's Secret Sex Trade
programme.desc: Nawal Al Maghafi exposes a secret world of sexual exploitation in Iraq, in which clerics use a controversial practice called pleasure marriage to groom vulnerable girls(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King mount their bikes for the final leg of their epic journey along the American road. They begin in Las Vegas, where they learn how to make apparently the best mashed potato in the world - inspiring them to cook a massive lobster and prawn cocktail. Back on the road they finally reach California, where they follow the path of the 49ers during the gold rush and learn about cooking with marijuana - which is perfectly legal in Los Angeles. They also enjoy the best tacos they have ever eaten at a taco festival, before reaching Santa Monica pier - the end of their trip(n)

programme.title: Food: Truth or Scare
programme.desc: Chris Bavin discovers the truth behind the claims that red meat causes cancer, and Gloria Hunniford investigates whether a cup of tea could actually be the secret to curing it. Plus, Danny Crates delves into an ingredient that's in more than two thirds of all processed foods and asks if eating soya can make men infertile(n)

programme.title: The Empty Housing Scandal
programme.desc: In Kent, Matt Allwright investigates plans for a property lying empty for eight years since the death of its owner. Plus, in Derbyshire a historic derelict building brought back to life to provide much-needed homes for the community(n)

programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood takes on his toughest challenge yet, working as a paramedic in Austin, Texas. His mentor is Cassi Lydon, a veteran paramedic with 14 years' service(n)

programme.title: Shop Well for Less?
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern travel to Leigh-on-Sea, Essex, to meet Antonietta Tulino and her three children, hoping to help the family change their spending habits. In an effort to confront their money-spending ways, Alex and Steph resort to shock tactics, and set up a pop-up-shop in the family home displaying all of their biggest spends, with shocking results. Can Alex and Steph help this family start saving, rather than spending?(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Sylvia Syms narrates a profile of the American actress, who died in December. Debbie became a mainstay of Hollywood's so-called 'golden age' with her appearance in Singin' in the Rain, and the programme makes use of rare archive interviews in which the star discusses her favourite film roles. Also covered is Debbie's private life, including the collapse of her marriage to Eddie Fisher, and her relationship with her daughter Carrie Fisher, who also died in December, one day before her mother(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Documentary examining 1954 film On the Waterfront, featuring archive interviews with the director, writer and cast members, including Marlon Brando. The programme also investigates how it was inspired by America's 1950s anti-communist culture and how stories of betrayal and resentment lie behind the movie's success. Narrated by Sylvia Syms(n)

programme.title: Live Tennis: ATP World Tour Finals
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day four from The O2 in London, featuring a singles match in the round-robin stage. Serbia's Novak Djokovic was at his dominant best in this session last year, defeating Alexander Zverev 6-4, 6-1. However, the German was to later gain revenge by overcoming Djokovic 6-4, 6-3 in the final(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball presents more backstage gossip and exclusive news from Strictly Come Dancing. Plus, Ian Waite casts his eye over the couples' latest training footage(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Show
programme.desc: Guests join Andrew Neil for interviews, discussion and analysis from Westminster, including the latest news on Brexit(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: The crew at Tower in central London, the busiest of the RNLI's 238 stations, are called to a man reported in the Thames eight miles downstream. On arrival, they discover a river bus already on the scene and their crew desperately trying to hang on to the unmoving man before he disappears under the water. At one of the most remote stations, Trearddur Bay on Anglesey, the crew are called to an inaccessible coastal fishing spot, where an angler has plunged face first down a cliff onto rocks, while in Portsmouth, gale force winds and a churning sea make a routine rescue particularly v(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The first three chefs recreate Monica's razor clams with a shallot, caper and tomato sauce, before the second group of three take on Marcus' calves liver with a Bordelaise sauce. In the next round, the six chefs aim to impress the judges with a dish of their own design, with just one hour and 15 minutes to prepare it(n)

programme.title: Guilt
programme.desc: Angie and Jake's budding relationship hits a problem when he begins to have suspicions about her, although she soon explains away his worries. Instead, she plants doubts in his mind about why his brother controls the finances of his record shop. Is there more to the business than it first appears? Thriller, starring Mark Bonnar, Jamie Sives and Ruth Bradley(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice: You're Fired
programme.desc: The companion programme to The Apprentice, with Tom Allen talking to the seventh candidate to be ejected from the competition. The host is also joined by a panel of experts and fans, who offer an entertaining analysis of this week's task(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels across Central America, from the Caribbean to the Pacific. Along the way, he goes diving on a coral reef on the coast of Belize, and witnesses a gun battle between park rangers and intruders in Chiquibul National Park. He also discovers how climate change has left Guatemala devastated by famine and drought, reports on measures to curb gang violence in El Salvador, and learns how Costa Rica aims to become the world's first carbon neutral country by 2021(n)

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The explorer travels from Texas to southern Mexico, meeting a woman who risks her life to take supplies to migrants stranded on the Mexican side of the border in Reynosa, one of the country's most violent cities, which is dominated by a powerful criminal organisation. He also ventures into the rainforest to explore the Maya city of Yaxchilan, and meets the modern-day descendants of this civilisation, who are increasingly marginalised and treated as second-class citizens(n)

programme.title: Inside the Vatican
programme.desc: A film crew gains exceptional access to Vatican, afforded a year to examine its inner workings. Cameras capture everyone including Pope Francis himself, his head of security, the nuns who cater for the homeless, the choristers of the Sistine Chapel, the papal gardener and the chief of the diplomatic corps to gain a unique and exclusive insight into the lives of those who live and work in this independent City State. The first of two programmes follows the build-up to Easter, when tens of thousands of pilgrims arrive and Pope Francis takes centre stage in the world's biggest theatre of faith(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Panellists Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani, Sara Davies and Theo Paphitis assess the profit-making potential of an inventive new solution to clearing up post-festival rubbish, a means of balancing gut bacteria from a sauerkraut enthusiast, and a new kind of phone alarm app. Presented by Evan Davis(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A profile of the screen career of John Hurt, the celebrated British character actor who died last month, having made a name for himself via roles in films such as The Elephant Man, The Naked Civil Servant, Midnight Express and 1984. In a selection of rare archive interviews, John discusses some of his most iconic cinematic creations, his perspective on acting, and the tragedy that claimed the life of the woman he planned to marry(n)

programme.desc: Charlie Ross and Will Axon assess items at Ragley Hall in Warwickshire, including a Busby hat and a Worcester coffee set. Presenter Paul Martin explores the Battle of Edgehill(n)

programme.desc: Jay Blades, Shaun Keaveny, Rachel Parris and Jan Ravens test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball talks to more couples ahead of the annual Blackpool show and has a sneak peek at a few of the costumes for the Tower Ballroom extravaganza with head designer Vicky Gill(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Actor Neil Morrissey and his friend the TV presenter JJ Chalmers search for bargains in Cheshire and Staffordshire, with the help of Izzie Balmer and Charles Hanson. Along the way, Neil has a go at some elaborate ceramic decoration, while JJ gets dressed up as a cow and joins a troupe of dancing villagers(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The six strongest chefs from the heats compete in the quarter-final, inventing a dish with onion as the key ingredient. After the weakest two are sent home, the others cook two courses for three critics - William Sitwell, Tracey MacLeod and Amol Rajan. However, judges Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing will ultimately decide who deserves a place in the next round(n)

programme.title: Giri/Haji
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the gunfight, Kenzo learns that Yuto's shrapnel-ridden body has been taken to Sarah's flat, so he, Vickers and Donna rush there and attempt makeshift surgery in order to save his brother's life. But also under the knife is Fukuhara in Tokyo, his bullet wounds threatening to crumble the very foundations of the Yakuza peace treaty. Crime thriller, starring Takehiro Hira, Yuko Nakamora and Kelly Macdonald(n)

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Ronnie Wood makes his debut on the show, with his Wild Five Band featuring Imelda May on vocals. Scottish chart-topper Lewis Capaldi sings tracks from his album Divinely Uninspired to a Hellish Extent and Oxford rockers Foals make a welcome return to the studio after a busy year. Completing the line-up are singer-songwriter Nilufer Yanya and violinist, singer and songwriter Sudan Archives(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: A Dangerous Dynasty: House of Assad
programme.desc: As a glamorous profile of Asma appears in Vogue magazine, the Arab Spring protests reach Syria. Bashar must decide whether to concede to their demands for greater freedom or follow in his father Hafez's famously hardline steps. What unfolds shocks the world in its brutality, but as the chaos of civil war rages, the so-called Islamic State emerges and it looks like Bashar is on the brink of losing the Assad dynasty's control of Syria(n)

programme.title: Live International Women's Football
programme.desc: Czech Republic v England (Kick-off 7.15pm). All the action from the friendly at Stadion Strelecky ostrov in Ceske Budejovice. This match comes just three days after the Lionesses faced Germany at Wembley, and head coach Phil Neville may opt to give run-outs to a number of fringe players who will be eager to impress against a team ranked 23 places below the visitors in the FIFA rankings(n)

programme.title: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: Vanessa Collingridge examines the life of Elizabeth Tudor, with a focus on how documentary TV and the BBC have looked at her legacy and interrogated her reign. Using archive stretching back over 60 years, her upbringing, her conflicts with enemies including Mary Queen of Scots, and her greatest victory against the Spanish Armada are investigated. The programme seeks to understand how the monarch created a legacy that people still live with today, and how it has changed throughout the centuries(n)

programme.title: Scuffles, Swagger & Shakespeare: The Hidden Story of English
programme.desc: John Gallagher examines how English became the world's most widely spoken language, examining the impact of William Shakespeare. During the playwright's lifetime, English was rarely spoken outside of Britain - but the beginnings of the British Empire would see it become a global language. The programme also examines what life was like for immigrants to Elizabethan Britain(n)

programme.title: Sleuths, Spies & Sorcerers: Andrew Marr's Paperback Heroes
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores the genres of fiction that are the most popular with the general public, unpicking their conventions to show why they are such page-turners. He begins by looking at detective novels, profiling pioneers Agatha Christie, Dashiell Hammett and Ruth Rendell, and decoding set-pieces such as Hercule Poirot's drawing room denouements and the locked room mysteries of John Dickson Carr. With contributions by Ian Rankin, Sophie Hannah, Anthony Horowitz and Val McDermid(n)

programme.title: Make! Craft Britain
programme.desc: A programme celebrating all things handmade, following ordinary people as they learn new skills taught and take part in craft workshops led by inspirational teachers. In the first episode, two groups of novice crafters master the art of hooky rugmaking and traditional letterpress. Elsewhere, origami artist Sam Tsang demonstrates how to make something beautiful using a single sheet of paper(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: in February, 2013, Jeff Bush was asleep at his home in Florida when a sink hole opened up beneath his bedroom. Despite the efforts of his brother to rescue him, Jeff was never seen alive again and his body could not be recovered. Professor Iain Stewart travels to the American state to discover what took Jeff's life and investigate why the geology of the area makes it the sinkhole capital of the world(n)

programme.title: Everything Is Connected - George Eliot's Life
programme.desc: Contemporary artist Gillian Wearing celebrates the legacy of Victorian novelist George Eliot. Just as Eliot's novel Middlemarch explored the lives of ordinary men and women, this experimental film is made up of a diverse cast of people from different backgrounds, and features Jason Isaacs and Sheila Atim as the narrators(n)

programme.title: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: Vanessa Collingridge examines the life of Elizabeth Tudor, with a focus on how documentary TV and the BBC have looked at her legacy and interrogated her reign. Using archive stretching back over 60 years, her upbringing, her conflicts with enemies including Mary Queen of Scots, and her greatest victory against the Spanish Armada are investigated. The programme seeks to understand how the monarch created a legacy that people still live with today, and how it has changed throughout the centuries(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: World War One at Home
programme.desc: Author Philip Hoare tells the story of the Royal Victoria Hospital in Netley, Hampshire, which treated at least 50,000 patients during the First World War. He also examines evidence that footage of cures for shellshock was faked(n)

programme.title: Six Wives with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian explores the fate of the last three wives of Henry VIII. First, Lucy witnesses Henry's doomed courtship with Anne of Cleves, with Henry successfully reneging on their marriage having claimed it had yet to be consummated. Two weeks later, Henry married teenager Catherine Howard, but an act of adultery with a courtier saw the young woman convicted of treason and beheaded. Finally, Lucy explores Henry's relationship with his last wife Katherine Parr, and how she managed to win Henry round despite their greatly differing religious beliefs(n)

programme.title: Britain's Lost Masterpieces
programme.desc: In Cardiff Art Gallery, the experts investigate a badly overpainted Madonna. Elsewhere, Dr Bendor Grosvenor travels to Florence to explore the early Renaissance(n)

programme.title: An Art Lovers' Guide
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez and Alastair Sooke explore the art and architecture of the Russian city, taking in a selection of imperial, communist and modern-day treasures(n)

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: For decades Margaret Atwood has been universally acclaimed as Canada's greatest living writer and at the age of 77 her star shines brighter and bolder than ever, thanks to a TV adaptation of her dystopian novel The Handmaid's Tale. In this edition of the arts documentary, Alan Yentob meets the author in Toronto to discover how a childhood spent between the wilderness and the city helped shape her vision of herself and the world, set alight her imagination and set her forth on a path to literary success(n)

programme.title: Relics: Treasures of Heaven
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon discovers how some of Christianity's most important relics have been preserved, and explores the art of creating reliquaries - containers for storing sacred items. He learns why some believe such objects have the power to work miracles, and views three reliquaries that contain articles associated with the crucifixion of Jesus Christ. Featuring contributions by art historian Sister Wendy Beckett and Neil MacGregor of the British Museum(n)

programme.title: The Renaissance Unchained
programme.desc: Waldemar Januszczak focuses on the impact on the period of Venice, which was a meeting point between east and west. He looks at how the exciting colours of Bellini, the mystery of Giorgione, the brilliance of Titian, the drama of Tintoretto and the elegance of Veronese created a new set of Renaissance rules - and produced inventive and visually stunning art(n)

programme.title: The Renaissance Unchained
programme.desc: Waldemar Januszczak examines the surprising climax of the Renaissance, a period full of war, confusion and darkness, which was captured perfectly in the art of Leonardo, Bosch, Arcimboldo, Palissy, the Italian mannerists and El Greco(n)

programme.title: Britain's Lost Masterpieces
programme.desc: In Cardiff Art Gallery, the experts investigate a badly overpainted Madonna. Elsewhere, Dr Bendor Grosvenor travels to Florence to explore the early Renaissance(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The Rosetta mission to comet 67P was the first time a spacecraft landed on a comet's surface. Cameras reveal what this icy body taught us about the dawn of the solar system and the origins of life on Earth(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Solar System
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox embarks on a globe-trotting quest to explain how the galactic forces that formed the solar system also shaped Earth's landscape. In the first programme, he examines the sun's rule over the planet, heading for India to witness a total solar eclipse, and viewing the Northern Lights in the Arctic Circle(n)

programme.title: Climategate: Science of a Scandal
programme.desc: Documentary investigating a data breach at the University of East Anglia in 2009 that suggested scientists had manipulated data to exaggerate evidence of climate change(n)

programme.title: Unlocking Nature's Secrets: The Serengeti Rules
programme.desc: Five veteran scientists tell the stories of their adventures in the wilderness going back to the 1960s, when their insatiable curiosity for the way nature worked took them to some of the most spectacular and remote places on Earth, from the majestic Serengeti and the Amazon jungle to the Arctic Ocean and Pacific tide pools(n)

programme.title: Climate Change by Numbers
programme.desc: The debate surrounding climate change, as the public appears to be less sure it understands exactly what is going on, despite there being more information on the issue than ever. Mathematicians Dr Hannah Fry, Professor Norman Fenton and Professor David Spiegelhalter discuss the three key numbers that clarify the important questions around climate change, as scientists use them to help explain what is happening to the Earth(n)

programme.title: Teacup Travels
programme.desc: Great Aunt Lizzie tells the story of Charlotte and the Adventure of the two identical Inro. Charlotte sees a mix up at the market when a chef and a writer accidently pick up each other's inro, the little cases that contain all their precious things. Thinking she has miso paste, Chef Machi adds ink to the soup she is making for a Samurai's dinner party. Can Charlotte make things right again? First she has to find the writer(n)

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: When Bob mixes up a delivery of cakes with a delivery of fish, Mrs Juniper is forced to sell the fish. James and the kids help Mrs Juniper turn her cafe into a fish shop(n)

programme.title: Melody
programme.desc: After visiting a bird park, Melody is introduced to The Firebird Suite by Igor Stravinsky, and her imagination takes her into a story about a firebird being held captive by a mean ogre(n)

programme.title: Biggleton
programme.desc: A mysterious squeak follows Odd Job Austin all around Biggleton. Narrated by Eamonn Holmes(n)

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Nursery adventures with Shaun the Sheep's baby cousin(n)

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say 'car', heralding a whirlwind word adventure(n)

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: The animated adventures of friends who can always count on each other. Find out what happens when numbers come to life and how they really work and play with the Numberblocks(n)

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: A three-year-old bunny meets friends and experiences emotions as he engages with the world around him(n)

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos watch a man practising his tennis strokes using a machine to shoot the balls to him, which prompts them to learn more about speed(n)

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friend become animal keepers at an animal park. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble looks after an animal too - his friend, Spotty the horse(n)

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies find different uses for their favourite things and a child in Tummy Tales makes a seaside picture out of lots of things from a make-and-do box. In Teletubbyland a large tube appears. The Teletubbies use it as a telescope and as a loud speaker(n)

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear meet babies Isobel, Idris, Lincoln, Amber, Amelia, Jason and Willow.(n)

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: A little boy journeys from real-life into the animated art world he creates, where his friends help him handle situations that make him feel anxious(n)

programme.title: Time for School
programme.desc: Observational documentary on the lives of reception children at two contrasting primary schools - one rural, and one urban(n)

programme.title: Andy's Dinosaur Adventures
programme.desc: Andy Day travels back 220 million years to the time of the coelophysis - one of the earliest dinosaurs that hunted in packs and was an expert at fishing(n)

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Cook creates the most delicious soup anyone has ever tasted, and Captain Captain orders him to make more so they can sell it and become rich(n)

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Energetic animation for children aged two to five, following Duggee the dog and his Squirrel Club(n)

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: Messy and friends have to play the Okido concert. The music is instrumental in nature, but then the time arrives for Okido to make some noise. Science-educational series(n)

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie Moate visits a fairground and goes for a ride on a carousel using a special camera to find out how it works. An animation shows how the carousel goes round and how the crankshaft works to move the horses up and down. Next, Maddie visits a factory to see how different coloured wax crayons are made from paraffin oil and how a big mold creates hundreds of crayons in one go(n)

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Benjamin gets a pet snail, but his friends cannot see the appeal, especially when it attracts the attention of Old Brown(n)

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters set out to stop Glitch playing dominoes with the ancient stones of Stonehenge(n)

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Evie's mum, Jess has just married Doug's Dad, Simon. They arrive home to find a puppy hiding in the wedding gifts. This magical, talking dog will change their lives for ever(n)

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Revival of the classic animation about the adventures of a clan of friendly aliens living on a small blue planet. Michael Palin narrates(n)

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Upsy Daisy is keen to look at the flowers in the garden, but Igglepiggle would rather ride in the Ninky Nonk(n)

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Annette Badland reads Not My Hats! by Tracy Gunaratnam and Alea Marley(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood takes on his toughest challenge yet, working as a paramedic in Austin, Texas. His mentor is Cassi Lydon, a veteran paramedic with 14 years' service(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a two-bedroom flat with rental potential in Catford, a terraced house in Dover, and a quirky terraced house in Accrington(n)

programme.title: Armchair Britain
programme.desc: Miriam Margolyes offers a guide around South Wales and the Valleys, visiting the beach at Barry before going for a spin in a Welsh sports car(n)

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: A massive police operation in Liverpool leads to a series of arrests of criminals, all of whom have made money illegally, but now the police want it back. We also see how Asset Recovery teams in the South West arrest a drugs gang and then seize the designer contents of their wardrobe - literally taking the shirts off their backs. Plus we meet the boxing club in Tuebrook who have had funding from money local police took off criminals(n)

programme.title: X-Ray
programme.desc: Lucy Owen finds out what is putting people off buying electric cars, while Omar Hamdi asks why customers who sign up for a Tastecard find it so difficult to cancel. Rachel Treadaway-Williams meets a couple who thought they were paying to reserve a property - only to discover they were victims of a scam(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The bargain hunters join Tim Wonnacott at the world-famous Portobello Road market and pick up an interesting collection of items, helped by experts James Braxton and Thomas Plant. But will they make any profit at auction?(n)

programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Karen prepares for a risky future, Daniel realises he cannot move forward without Zara's help, and Ayesha discovers her cousin Kayleigh is trying to step into her shoes(n)

programme.desc: By the Welsh Labour Party(n)

programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As the party continues, Sharon and Mel threaten to reveal each other's big secrets. While Lisa tries to make Mel see sense, Sharon sneaks out to meet Phil at the airport. Across the square, Chantelle bumps into Denise but is left confused when she mentions a fight Gray was involved in, and Linda and Mick take a trip down memory lane(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: A paranoid Jac feels she is the centre of hospital gossip when word spreads Emma is now living with Jonny. Elliot's concern for her grows, but feels unable to tell her his secret. Lofty resolves to put his new family first, but even the basics of parenting stir up trouble between him, Helen and Dom. Sacha struggles with his feelings for Essie, which seem doomed to go unrequited. Guest starring Vicky Entwistle (Coronation Street)(n)

programme.title: Gold Digger
programme.desc: Drama, starring Julia Ormond as a wealthy middle-aged woman who begins a relationship with a man nearly 20 years younger than she is just as her divorce is finalised. When he is introduced to her three adult children, suspicions are raised about his motivations. The action cuts between the early days of their relationship and the wedding day, which seems fated to end in disaster. With Ben Barnes and Jemima Rooper(n)

programme.title: Beauty Queen and Single
programme.desc: Working mum and Miss European runner up 2008 Sinita braves dating minus the make-up(n)

programme.title: Love and Hate Crime
programme.desc: Two murders in Houston, Texas, that left baffled police thinking there was an Iranian hit squad at work. Ali Isran, who was convicted of the crimes, tells his story from death row, and the film follows the extraordinary twists and turns of the case(n)

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the fifth episode, Jared and Sophie, a couple from Essex, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon(n)

programme.title: Earth from Space
programme.desc: Cameras in space reveal the stories behind Earth's most remarkable patterns and shapes, including fresh water lagoons in Brazil, mysterious light spots in the Australian outback, endless green canopy in the Congo rainforest, and the twists and turns of the Amazon river which provides a home for rehabilitated manatees(n)

A team of experts and volunteers come together to help transform an old church into a new accommodation facility for six young homeless people in Blackburn, ran by local charity Nightsafe(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: This week, the candidates are tasked to create advertising campaigns to entice people from the UK to visit Finland during the summer months. Half the team head to Finland to film an advert for television, whilst the others create a marketing campaign and a digital billboard to be displayed at London's Piccadilly circus. Next, the candidates pitch their campaigns to leading industry experts, including the Head of Tourism for Finland. Back in the boardroom, one of the candidates leaves the competition(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Live
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad and Bethan Rhys Roberts present a weekly show featuring stories and in-depth interviews about what is happening in Wales and why it matters(n)

programme.title: Why Dad Killed Mum: My Family's Secret
programme.desc: A young woman searches for the answers as to why her father killed her mother, grandmother and aunt in a house fire when she was just 16 months old. Tasnim, who is now 19, has been asked to bring her opinion to the parole board as her father nears the end of his 18-year jail sentence. Her search for the truth about her mother's murder takes her back to the late 1990s as she investigates her parents' relationship, and reveals a shocking truth that takes her deep into a secret that rocked a community(n)

programme.title: Why Dad Killed Mum: My Family's Secret
programme.desc: Tasnim gains accesses court transcripts from her father's trial, which reveal reports of possessive behaviour and sexual jealousy throughout her parents' turbulent relationship. Later, a childhood friend of her Tasnim's mother also claims that her father allegedly manipulated and groomed her and other underage girls(n)

programme.title: Why Dad Killed Mum: My Family's Secret
programme.desc: In the final episode, the police release Tasnim's mother's belongings for the first time, and among the items Tasnim makes a shocking discovery which highlights the true extent of the abuse she suffered at the hands of her father. Ahead of her father's parole hearing, Tasnim looks to the future and hopes that her family can finally find justice(n)

programme.title: Inside the Supermarket
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of Sainsbury's. After a challenging Christmas, the staff go all out to maximise profits with their flower sales over the Mother's Day weekend. Plans are also made to sell a birthday cake to celebrate the company's 150th year of trading, bringing in Claire Ptak, the creator of Harry and Meghan Markle's wedding cake, for ideas. Plus, last year CEO Mike Coupe announced a possible merger with Asda. Will industry regulators give the company the go-ahead to become the biggest supermarket in the country?(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood joins the coastguard of Corpus Christi, Texas, taking to the skies with a helicopter patrol and joining a boat crew looking for illegal fishing and smuggling(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development progress of properties sold at auction in Gorleston in Norfolk, Dover in Kent and Wolverhampton in the West Midlands(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Gang members wrongly think they can cash in by deliberately causing a collision with a bus, while a holidaymaker's claim for lost luggage is grounded thanks to forged documents(n)

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Thames Valley Police pursue suspect fraudsters in their region and give one man a very early morning call. programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Motorcycling great Carl Fogarty, England netball star Eboni Usoro-Brown, Olympic hockey gold medallist Helen Richardson-Walsh and world champion swimmer Mark Foster join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: The Wedding Dress Shop
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the front door of a wedding dress shop where designer Samantha Buca aims to make all brides-to-be look fabulous. programme.title: The Met: Policing London
programme.desc: In Newham, east London, the Trident team is targeting one of the capital's most dangerous gangs, whose leaders groom school children to commit violent acts of crime - and DCI Jim McKee gets a lead after the kidnap and torture of a teenage boy. New Scotland Yard appeal for witnesses to come forward when a man falls victim to an attack in Trafalgar Square, and dies later from his injuries. In Ealing, response officers Naomi Pearce and Mahad Abdalla deal with a drunk-driver(n)

Half the team head to Finland to film an advert for television, whilst the others create a marketing campaign and a digital billboard to be displayed at London's Piccadilly circus. Next, the candidates pitch their campaigns to leading industry experts, including the Head of Tourism for Finland. Back in the boardroom, one of the candidates leaves the competition(n)

programme.title: Why Dad Killed Mum: My Family's Secret
programme.desc: A young woman searches for the answers as to why her father killed her mother, grandmother and aunt in a house fire when she was just 16 months old. Tasnim, who is now 19, has been asked to bring her opinion to the parole board as her father nears the end of his 18-year jail sentence. Her search for the truth about her mother's murder takes her back to the late 1990s as she investigates her parents' relationship, and reveals a shocking truth that takes her deep into a secret that rocked a community(n)

programme.title: Why Dad Killed Mum: My Family's Secret
programme.desc: Tasnim gains accesses court transcripts from her father's trial, which reveal reports of possessive behaviour and sexual jealousy throughout her parents' turbulent relationship. Later, a childhood friend of her Tasnim's mother also claims that her father allegedly manipulated and groomed her and other underage girls(n)

programme.title: Why Dad Killed Mum: My Family's Secret
programme.desc: In the final episode, the police release Tasnim's mother's belongings for the first time, and among the items Tasnim makes a shocking discovery which highlights the true extent of the abuse she suffered at the hands of her father. Ahead of her father's parole hearing, Tasnim looks to the future and hopes that her family can finally find justice(n)

programme.title: Inside the Supermarket
programme.desc: As more people worry about the use of single-use plastic, cameras follow the Sainsbury's innovation team as they try to find a recyclable alternative for the plastic bag used to carry loose fruit and vegetables. But as it is launched across the country, are customers ready for such a change in shopping habits? With Easter around the corner, the supply staff decide what to stock in each store and how much, while at the clothing HQ, plans are already being drawn up for this year's Christmas fashion(n)

programme.title: Secret Body
programme.desc: Undercover body transformation programme in which lifestyle and fitness experts Rab and Stephen team up with two volunteers who want to lose weight and improve their fitness. However, there's a twist. Their loved ones will know nothing about it as 30-year-old make-up artist Ashley from Aberdeen and 52-year-old singing cabbie Wayne will be wearing specially made body suits to ensure they look the same throughout the entire process(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Brighton, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: The TV version of the popular radio show taking a light-hearted view of all things related to Brexit and beyond, with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason engaging in banter about the ongoing process to leave the EU(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a two-bedroom flat with rental potential in Catford, a terraced house in Dover, and a quirky terraced house in Accrington(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A driver's personal injury claim is rumbled when CCTV footage reveals that he was never even in the car, and an opportunistic bus passenger comes unstuck(n)

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: A massive police operation in Liverpool leads to a series of arrests of criminals, all of whom have made money illegally, but now the police want it back. We also see how Asset Recovery teams in the South West arrest a drugs gang and then seize the designer contents of their wardrobe - literally taking the shirts off their backs. Plus we meet the boxing club in Tuebrook who have had funding from money local police took off criminals(n)

programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As the party continues, Sharon and Mel threaten to reveal each other's big secrets. While Lisa tries to make Mel see sense, Sharon sneaks out to meet Phil at the airport. Across the square, Chantelle bumps into Denise but is left confused when she mentions a fight Gray was involved in, and Linda and Mick take a trip down memory lane(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: A paranoid Jac feels she is the centre of hospital gossip when word spreads Emma is now living with Jonny. Elliot's concern for her grows, but feels unable to tell her his secret. Lofty resolves to put his new family first, but even the basics of parenting stir up trouble between him, Helen and Dom. Sacha struggles with his feelings for Essie, which seem doomed to go unrequited. Guest starring Vicky Entwistle (Coronation Street)(n)

programme.title: Gold Digger
programme.desc: Drama, starring Julia Ormond as a wealthy middle-aged woman who begins a relationship with a man nearly 20 years younger than she is just as her divorce is finalised. When he is introduced to her three adult children, suspicions are raised about his motivations. The action cuts between the early days of their relationship and the wedding day, which seems fated to end in disaster. With Ben Barnes and Jemima Rooper(n)

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.desc: Investigations into social and political issues affecting Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Love and Hate Crime
programme.desc: Two murders in Houston, Texas, that left baffled police thinking there was an Iranian hit squad at work. Ali Isran, who was convicted of the crimes, tells his story from death row, and the film follows the extraordinary twists and turns of the case(n)

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the fifth episode, Jared and Sophie, a couple from Essex, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon(n)

programme.title: Earth from Space
programme.desc: Cameras in space reveal the stories behind Earth's most remarkable patterns and shapes, including fresh water lagoons in Brazil, mysterious light spots in the Australian outback, endless green canopy in the Congo rainforest, and the twists and turns of the Amazon river which provides a home for rehabilitated manatees(n)

Inspired by the location of the Moseley family's final holiday together with Harry, the team transforms her back yard into an Ibizan-style hideaway full of happy reminders of Harry(n)

programme.title: A Very Royal Wedding
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong presents a documentary about the marriage of Elizabeth II and Prince Philip on November 20, 1947, featuring colour archive film of the wedding as well as clips from the Queen's private home movie footage. Joan Collins and Sheila Hancock are among those sharing their memories of the ceremony, and there are insights from those who were part of the big day, including Prince Michael of Kent, who was a page boy(n)

programme.title: England's Grand

programme.title: Back to Mine

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: After a mystery sea monster washes up on a UK beach, extreme angler Jeremy Wade sets off on a journey to unmask this beast and find out whether it poses any threat to humans. His journey takes him to London, where he digs for clues among celebrated naturalist Charles Darwin's historic specimens, to the frozen waters of Greenland, where he ice fishes deeper than he's ever fished before, and on to the fjords of Norway, where he attempts to reel in one of these sea monsters before an Arctic storm rolls in(n)

programme.title: Stephen Fry in Central America
programme.desc: Stephen travels to El Salvador and Honduras, two of Central America's most dangerous countries, ridden with gangs and drug cartels. However, El Salvador also has some of the best waves in the Americas, and the presenter is soon persuaded to try his hand at surfing. Moving on to Honduras, he visits a banana plantation where new varieties are being developed and meets a woman convicted of killing and cooking six of her husbands, before stepping over the border into Nicaragua for a visit to a cigar factory and to learn how to dance the maypole(n)

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: England v Montenegro (Kick-off 7.45pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the Group A qualifier at Wembley Stadium. This is England's 1,000th international match and they need at least a draw to mark the occasion by securing their place in the Euro 2020 finals. The reverse fixture saw Gareth Southgate's men claim an emphatic 5-1 victory, when the hosts took a shock lead before Michael Keane equalised, Ross Barkley scored a brace and the prolific duo of Harry Kane and Raheem Sterling added one each. With analysis from Roy Keane, Lee Dixon and Ashley Cole, and commentary by Clive Tyldesley and Glenn Hoddle(n)

programme.title: International Football Highlights
programme.desc: England v Montenegro. Mark Pougatch presents action from the Group A qualifier at Wembley Stadium. Although England's 100 per cent record in the group came to an end in the Czech Republic last month, they will have been expecting to claim at least the draw they needed to secure their place in the Euro 2020 finals with one match to spare. With analysis from Lee Dixon and Glenn Hoddle, and commentary by Clive Tyldesley(n)

programme.title: Out of Their Skin
programme.desc: Part one of two. Ian Wright charts the inspiring rise of black footballers in Britain, from Viv Anderson's debut as England's first senior black player in 1978, with the aid of archive footage, original interviews and a compilation of individual stories from the past and present. Contributors include Paul Ince, Ashley Cole, Les Ferdinand, John Barnes, Raheem Sterling and Marcus Rashford(n)

programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh heads to Birmingham to surprise Georgie Moseley - the founder of Britain's first cancer drop-in centre, which she set up following the death of her 11-year-old son Harry. Inspired by the location of the Moseley family's final holiday together with Harry, the team transforms her back yard into an Ibizan-style hideaway full of happy reminders of Harry(n)

programme.title: A Very Royal Wedding
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong presents a documentary about the marriage of Elizabeth II and Prince Philip on November 20, 1947, featuring colour archive film of the wedding as well as clips from the Queen's private home movie footage. Joan Collins and Sheila Hancock are among those sharing their memories of the ceremony, and there are insights from those who were part of the big day, including Prince Michael of Kent, who was a page boy(n)

programme.title: England's Grand
programme.desc: Gareth Southgate, Harry Kane and Ian Wright join a host of players, authors and experts for an A-Z journey through the history of the England football team. The documentary brings together previously unheard stories, anecdotes and unearthed archive in a celebration of the 147 years leading up to England's 1,000th match, which takes place on Thursday 14th November against Montenegro(n)

programme.title: Back to Mine
programme.desc: Bachelor Cal goes looking for love by checking out the houses of three potential dates in the North West - and this former professional footballer appears to have a huge choice when it comes to picking one to take out on a date. There is a pop music lover, a fitness fanatic and the 'Beyonce of the caravan park'. But which one will he want to score a date with?(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: After a mystery sea monster washes up on a UK beach, extreme angler Jeremy Wade sets off on a journey to unmask this beast and find out whether it poses any threat to humans. His journey takes him to London, where he digs for clues among celebrated naturalist Charles Darwin's historic specimens, to the frozen waters of Greenland, where he ice fishes deeper than he's ever fished before, and on to the fjords of Norway, where he attempts to reel in one of these sea monsters before an Arctic storm rolls in(n)

programme.title: Stephen Fry in Central America
programme.desc: Stephen travels to El Salvador and Honduras, two of Central America's most dangerous countries, ridden with gangs and drug cartels. However, El Salvador also has some of the best waves in the Americas, and the presenter is soon persuaded to try his hand at surfing. Moving on to Honduras, he visits a banana plantation where new varieties are being developed and meets a woman convicted of killing and cooking six of her husbands, before stepping over the border into Nicaragua for a visit to a cigar factory and to learn how to dance the maypole(n)

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: England v Montenegro (Kick-off 7.45pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the Group A qualifier at Wembley Stadium. This is England's 1,000th international match and they need at least a draw to mark the occasion by securing their place in the Euro 2020 finals. The reverse fixture saw Gareth Southgate's men claim an emphatic 5-1 victory, when the hosts took a shock lead before Michael Keane equalised, Ross Barkley scored a brace and the prolific duo of Harry Kane and Raheem Sterling added one each. With analysis from Roy Keane, Lee Dixon and Ashley Cole, and commentary by Clive Tyldesley and Glenn Hoddle(n)

programme.title: International Football Highlights
programme.desc: England v Montenegro. Mark Pougatch presents action from the Group A qualifier at Wembley Stadium. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective's friend Sir Charles Cartwright (guest star Martin Shaw) hosts a cocktail party at his home in Cornwall, where an amiable local priest chokes to death on his drink. There seems to be no motive for murder, and no trace of poison found in the victim's glass, but weeks later, when Sir Charles's doctor friend Sir Bartholomew Strange dies in similar circumstances, he and Poirot team up to investigate. Drama, with Art Malik, Jane Asher and Anna Carteret, and David Suchet in the title role(n)

programme.desc: Farmer Alex Preston is found dead at the sacred Crowcall stone circle in Midsomer Mow. Suspicion initially falls on local druids led by high priests Ezra Canning and Leticia Clifford, since Preston's plans to plough Gorse Meadow would have barred their access to the site. Barnaby and Jones are joined by new pathologist Kate Wilding to investigate, and as the case wears on, the inspector becomes convinced greed rather than spiritualism is the motive. Starring Neil Dudgeon, Jason Hughes and Tamzin Malleson(n)

When it appears that the case is closed, Banks and the team are are faced with an unexpected turn of events. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, Andrea Lowe, Caroline Catz and Jack Deam(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective's friend Sir Charles Cartwright (guest star Martin Shaw) hosts a cocktail party at his home in Cornwall, where an amiable local priest chokes to death on his drink. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is reunited with sidekick Captain Hastings (Hugh Fraser), secretary Miss Lemon (Pauline Moran) and Inspector Japp (Philip Jackson) in a case that plunges him into the world of global espionage as the Second World War looms. The public are in panic after the shocking death of Russian grandmaster Ivan Savaranoff during a game of chess. Poirot must try to determine the good guys from the bad in a complex plot that sees the main figures used like pawns. David Suchet stars in a story adapted by Mark Gatiss and Ian Hallard(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Midsomer Parva hosts the revival of a traditional pagan festival, but tragedy strikes when curate Alex Dakin is burned alive inside a straw effigy of a woman. The body count rises as an apparent victim of spontaneous combustion, a budding photographer and a newly arrived schoolteacher are the next to be roasted alive. Keith Barron guest stars(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When a toddler goes missing from a high-street merry-go-round, his distraught mother reports him kidnapped and a massive police hunt is launched to track him down. However, with more than one suspect and no physical evidence, the search throws up more questions than answers - especially as the boy's mum seems to be hiding something. Crime drama, starring Bradley Walsh and Jamie Bamber(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A call girl is found murdered at an upmarket apartment, apparently the victim of a client who risks losing everything if his secret obsession with the woman is revealed. Guest starring Greg Wise, with Bradley Walsh and Freema Agyeman(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A serious assault leads the police to foil an attempt to steal antiques from Ashfordly Hall, and Vernon goes all-out to persuade a wealthy woman to make a charitable contribution toward his donkey sanctuary - but the discovery that there is already one in the area forces him to change his plans. Meanwhile, Merton devises a solution to Ventress's retirement worries. Rosemary Leach guest stars(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Carol is on the trail of Turner's secret, Mark discovers he has gone up in the world, and Jack finds he cannot resist Annie's powers of persuasion(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris is tormented by memories, Nick's outburst troubles Elizabeth, and Carol disturbs an intimate moment(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Reg and Maureen set up shop, Alma is shaken when Tanya asks her not to let herself into the flat anymore, and Vera agrees to print Mike's T-shirts(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Derek arranges a surprise day trip to London for Mavis, Tanya tells Alma to keep her nose out of her private life, and Maud is upset when an offer is made on her house(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is reunited with old friend Ariadne Oliver, who has been commissioned to stage a game of murder hunt at a summer fete in the grounds of Nasse House, the recently purchased home of wealthy financier Sir George Stubbs. However, the novelist's instincts tell her she's getting involved in something much darker - and she's proved right when the girl volunteering to play the part of the victim really does turn out to be dead. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Zoe Wanamaker, Sean Pertwee, Martin Jarvis, Sam Kelly, Sinead Cusack, Rosalind Ayres and Tom Ellis(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A serious assault leads the police to foil an attempt to steal antiques from Ashfordly Hall, and Vernon goes all-out to persuade a wealthy woman to make a charitable contribution toward his donkey sanctuary - but the discovery that there is already one in the area forces him to change his plans. Meanwhile, Merton devises a solution to Ventress's retirement worries. Rosemary Leach guest stars(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper sleuth returns to his home country of Belgium for the first time since the First World War to attend a medal ceremony, only to find a murder case needs solving. Agatha Christie mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Chancellor and David De Keyser(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The detective is faced with a difficult case when he investigates the discovery of a body in a well, and he also has to contend with a new boss, Chief Superintendent Moody, who is dubious as to Lewis' motivation for coming out of retirement. As investigations into the human remains in the well proceed, an avant-garde artist's body is also discovered and at first glance it looks like a tragic drugs overdose, but Hathaway and Lewis quickly recognise the tell tale signs of murder(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The start of a shooting rampage through the streets of London leaves a nightclub peppered with bullets and two revellers dead. With the media swarming all over the case and a hostage to track down, Brooks and Devlin race against the clock to find the duo responsible for the massacre, but their defence barrister (Lesley Manville) claims one of them was more innocent victim than violent killer. With Brooke Kinsella(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin are mystified by the murders of a popular couple who were stabbed to death while they slept. Lacking any leads to possible grievances held or enemies made, the detectives suspect a case of mistaken identity, and the previous owner of the house - a banker caught up in a hedge-fund scandal - emerges as the more likely target. Guest starring Samuel West(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally is furious that Kevin gave away the picture. Reg has a lonely stag night as all his pals cancel on him. Maureen's friends try to put her off the wedding at the Rovers(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Des takes Raquel to the wedding, Reg fears Maureen will jilt him, Mike is amazed when Steve offers him a partnership at the print shop, and Sally asks Emily for the picture back(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris is upset when Kathy's birthday plans do not include him. Turner and Frank settle their differences. Nick is horrified when he discovers what is wrong with Peter Whiteley(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Annie is delighted at Sarah's news, but wants to see her made an 'honest woman'. Kathy admits that her marriage is over. Joe tells his stepchildren that he is finalising a divorce(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dr Liz Merrick is mortified when one of her patients dies of an insulin overdose, but even more so when Sgt Merton is forced to investigate her responsibility. Meanwhile, Vernon Scripps embarks on a mission to make Aidensfield sparkle with his own brand of cleaning products. Drama, starring Aislin McGuckin and Duncan Bell(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Two women vie for the inspector's affections convinced the stars foretell love. Classic Seventies comedy, starring Stephen Lewis and Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Inspector Blake gives Jack a new clippie, knowing her incessant chatter will drive him barmy. Seventies comedy, starring Bob Grant and Stephen Lewis(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Owing to a little white lie on his income tax returns, Mr Roper must quickly acquire a son. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: In Dover, Lieutenant Colin Race is investigating the deaths of two Navy personnel when a distraught Sheila Webb runs out of 19 Wilbraham Crescent and into his arms. Inside the house, Colin finds the body of a murder victim surrounded by stopped clocks. Mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Massey, Jaime Winstone, Lesley Sharp, Phil Daniels, Geoffrey Palmer and Tessa Peake-Jones(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Steve comes to the aid of an amnesiac struggling to piece together his identity, Jenny tells Dennis she is tired of playing second fiddle to his career, and Bernie and David step in when it appears Vernon's money-making schemes are getting the better of him. Rural drama, starring James Carlton, Ian East, Peter Benson, David Lonsdale and Geoffrey Hughes(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jayesh is sticking to his guns, Chris is scraping the barrel, and at the Woolpack, a wine tasting leaves Turner in low spirits(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim has a small visitor, Viv discovers the size of Scott's tall tales while in Leeds, and Zoe's enthusiasm for poetry is short-lived(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maud is upset she has spoilt the honeymoon, Alma fumes when Mike plans to take Mark away on a fishing trip, and Bet spends a night in a hotel with Charlie(n)

programme.desc: Reg and Maureen go off on their honeymoon, Audrey is annoyed when Alf refuses to spend money now they are retired, and Denise has the picture valued(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth sets out to solve the baffling murder of a Cambridge don who met with foul play shortly after deciding to change his will. Starring David Suchet, Beth Goddard and Mark Kingston(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Farmer Alex Preston is found dead at the sacred Crowcall stone circle in Midsomer Mow. Suspicion initially falls on local druids led by high priests Ezra Canning and Leticia Clifford, since Preston's plans to plough Gorse Meadow would have barred their access to the site. Barnaby and Jones are joined by new pathologist Kate Wilding to investigate, and as the case wears on, the inspector becomes convinced greed rather than spiritualism is the motive. Starring Neil Dudgeon, Jason Hughes and Tamzin Malleson(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. The search for Xun Li, a missing devoted husband and father who has a successful property business, soon escalates into a kidnap and ransom case. However, with the perpetrators cleverly staying one step ahead of the police, it becomes clear that they are not dealing with average criminals. When it appears that the case is closed, Banks and the team are are faced with an unexpected turn of events. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, Andrea Lowe, Caroline Catz and Jack Deam(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: The team is thrown when a recently rescued kidnap victim is found murdered, with evidence suggesting a local protection gang is to blame. However, apprehending the woman they suspect proves challenging, as witnesses know the price they will pay for talking to the police. Meanwhile, Banks discovers a long-buried secret. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, Andrea Lowe and Caroline Catz(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) and four known killers are among those who meet Poirot at a bridge evening arranged by wealthy adventurer Shaitana, who sits out the first game - only to be found later stabbed through the heart. As nobody has left or entered the victim's apartment during the night, the guests combine their skills to discover which of the party is responsible. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Lesley Manville and Alex Jennings(n)

programme.desc: The inspector's mother comes to stay, and proves to be a thoroughly unpleasant person. Seventies comedy, starring Stephen Lewis and Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: The Professionals

programme.title: The Sweeney

programme.title: The Chase

programme.title: US Marshals (1998)
programme.desc: A federal marshal sets out in pursuit of an escaped prisoner who is determined to prove his murder conviction was false. As the chase progresses, the marshal is forced to work with a diplomatic security agent, making him realise the fugitive is no ordinary convict and that his claims of innocence may be true. Thriller sequel, starring Tommy Lee Jones reprising his Oscar-winning role from The Fugitive, alongside Wesley Snipes and Robert Downey Jr(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Part one of two. A Chinese street gang launches an attack on members of a rival syndicate, knowing they will blame a third faction - which will leaving the city facing a gang war(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A man confesses to murder, thinking he has killed his boss, but the sleuthing medical examiner believes he is innocent and tries to get him off the hook(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: The lure of easy money beckons once again as Terry and Arthur appreciate the commercial possibilities of cheap tobacco, but it is not long before the would-be smugglers are all at sea. Comedy, starring George Cole, Dennis Waterman and Ray Winstone(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are asked to keep a surveillance on Cowley after evidence suggests he has stolen two canisters of a lethal nerve gas and begun negotiating with an unfriendly foreign power. The plot thickens when they trace their boss to a country mansion, where after they are caught he denies knowing them(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A £30,000 consignment of mercury is stolen and the squad is bemused when the prime suspect comes up with a cast-iron alibi - he was in the company of his 'mistress' hours before his planned wedding ceremony. Diana Dors guest stars in the crime drama, alongside John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Good Morning Britain's Ranvir Singh, journalist John Sergeant, ex-rugby international Ugo Monye and TV presenter Mariella Frostrup pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: Featuring a family farm making jam for the Queen's breakfast table, a Sheffield man producing classic safety razors, a bed-maker using recycled bottles to make mattresses, and a British clockmaker paying tribute to a man that changed the course of history. Ricky Tomlinson narrates(n)

programme.title: The Enforcer (1976)
programme.desc: Maverick cop 'Dirty' Harry Callahan is unwillingly teamed up with a female partner as he takes on terrorists who have stolen a shipment of weapons and explosives and plan to hold the city of San Francisco to ransom. Crime thriller sequel, starring Clint Eastwood, Tyne Daly, Harry Guardino, Bradford Dillman and John Mitchum(n)

programme.title: The Saint
