programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Part two of two. Chinese gangsters kidnap an ailing Italian mobster in retribution for being disregarded by the city's other crime syndicates, and demand a $2million ransom(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: An apparently open-and-shut homicide case looks like an easy day's work for Quincy, until he receives a request from an old friend pleading for his help(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur is repaid a debt in dodgy £20 notes, but by the time it comes to light they are counterfeit his creditor is heading on a flight to India. Once the Winchester is pinpointed as the source of the forged currency, Arthur also finds he has Chisholm breathing down his neck. Robbie Coltrane and Art Malik guest star, with George Cole and Patrick Malahide(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 is faced with a terrifying campaign of explosions and assassinations, apparently committed by an opposing operative that Cowley is convinced he killed several years earlier. It appears that he is either mistaken or an old associate of his former adversary is seeking revenge. Gordon Jackson stars(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A Scottish detective on the trail of a gang of crooks requests help, and the squad resolves to catch his quarry. However, it becomes apparent that the visiting sleuth is holding back some important information that Regan and his colleagues need to know. TV presenter Janet Ellis features in a supporting role(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as impressionist Rory Bremner, weather reporter Laura Tobin, Dragons' Den's Deborah Meaden and TV gardener and chat show host Alan Titchmarsh pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove visit an emporium full of trinketry, and look to make a profit at one of their favourite autojumbles(n)

programme.title: Death Race (2008)
programme.desc: Jensen Ames is framed for the murder of his wife and sent to Terminal Island Prison, a facility that broadcasts the highly profitable Death Race, a high-octane event where convicts battle to the death in customised cars. When a popular driver dies, crooked prison warden Hennessey persuades Ames to take his place, claiming he will be released if he is victorious. Sci-fi thriller, starring Jason Statham, Joan Allen, Ian McShane and Tyrese Gibson(n)

programme.title: The World's Greatest Bridges
programme.desc: Opened in 1966, the mile-long Severn Bridge was the first road bridge to connect England and south Wales by spanning the Severn estuary, one of the most dangerous waterways in Britain. An ill-fated 19th-century railway crossing had previously existed over the Severn, but it was demolished in the late 1960s after a number of fatal accidents. Rob Bell explores the failures in engineering that led to the downfall of that earlier bridge and reveals how designer Dr Bill Brown took a radically new approach by developing an aerodynamic box girder deck that completely revolutionised bridge design(n)

programme.title: The World's Greatest Bridges
programme.desc: At 343 metres, the Millau Viaduct over the Tarn gorge in southern France is the world's tallest bridge and considered by many to be the world's most beautiful. Engineer Rob Bell meets the design team, including architect Norman Foster and structural engineer Dr Michel Virlogeux, and learns about the innovative and ancient techniques used to build it. London's Millennium Bridge, Foster's pedestrian-only thoroughfare, is also featured, including what caused its infamous initial wobble and how it was rectified(n)

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of a high-speed police interception unit. Officers pursue an off-road motorbike, which results in the riders trying to escape over a garden fence. programme.title: Police Raids: Caught by Surprise
programme.desc: Cameras follow enforcement agencies carrying out raids across Britain, as they take drastic action against fraudsters, counterfeiters and criminals. Police tackle the brothers responsible for a major drug-dealing operation in south Wales, while sniffer dogs Yoyo and Scamp track illegal and possibly dangerous cigarettes(n)

programme.title: Robbing Your Relatives
programme.desc: The case of a man who repeatedly lied and stole from the woman he claimed to love, racking up thousands of pounds of debt by stealing her credit cards and inventing a fantasy life. This episode also examines the case of a family member who allegedly conned an elderly relative out of her life savings(n)

programme.title: Hunted and Confronted: Cowboys, Crooks and Chancers
programme.desc: Undercover reporter Paul Connolly presents a Trading Standards special, following investigation units across the UK as they raid high street shops. He also exposes the rogue traders who did not just do a bad job - they tried to steal an entire house(n)

programme.title: Heists
programme.desc: Documentary using testimonies from victims, police investigators and CCTV footage to detail the events of an armed robbery in February 2006, when criminals managed to steal £53million in cash from a Securitas depot in Tonbridge, Kent. The raid was so costly it threatened to undermine the UK's economic stability and saw the robbers take hostages, who were manipulated into helping them gain access to the vaults. The subsequent police investigation initially began as a hunt for a highly organised squad of professional robbers, but a series of catastrophic mistakes on the part of the culprits made it clear they were low-level criminals seeking riches(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Chief petty officer Leland Wiley dies of a heart attack just as he is about to reveal information on elusive arms dealer Agah Bayar. The team discovers the cardiac arrest was caused by someone hacking into a computer monitoring Wiley's pacemaker and increasing his heart rate to 400bpm. Meanwhile, a probationary agent tries to impress Gibbs and win a place on his team. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, with Tamer Hassan and Matt L Jones(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Confidential files are leaked by an anonymous hacker, who threatens to release more unless the Navy agrees to renew a contract with a struggling shipbuilding firm. Meanwhile, Gibbs' colleagues begin to notice his attraction to his opposite number Samantha Ryan. Guest starring Jamie Lee Curtis(n)

programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: Tim Marlow examines the work of Italian Renaissance painter Raffaello Santi, known better simply as Raphael, who was born in 1483 and achieved so much in his 37-year life that he was considered equal to Leonardo da Vinci and Michelangelo. He was highly regarded for his many studies of the Madonna and Child, and artists adopted his idealised forms as the model of beauty until the end of the 19th century(n)

Those taking part include food fanatic Hannah, who thinks nothing of filling up on chocolate spread straight from the jar, student Simon, who has kept going throughout his dissertation with the help of custard creams, sugary teas and carb-heavy rice dishes, and Olivia, who tops up with secret bedtime snacks(n)

programme.title: M&S vs Waitrose: Which Is Better Value?
programme.desc: Documentary examining the ongoing rivalry between the supermarkets. Marks & Spencer has always had a special place in the nation's heart, with almost 1,000 stores and a reputation for quality stretching back 135 years. But Waitrose has proved a fierce rival. However, both stores face tough challenges in the future, not least from online retailers. Which of the two is best equipped to weather the high-street storm?(n)

programme.title: Good Girls' Guide to Kinky Sex
programme.desc: Three self-confessed 'good girls' and their partners - held back by a fear of being adventurous in the bedroom - look for creative ways to improve their sex lives. programme.title: Gypsy Kids: Our Secret World
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old Romany gypsy Ben has been bullied at school as long as he can remember. Not to be held back, he has dreams of becoming a pilot, and funds his £150 lessons himself by selling eggs to the local cafe. But can he obtain his qualifications with so many interruptions to his education? Four-year-old Rudy watches his dad, professional cage fighter Tony, for the first time, and Irish traveller friends Alivia and Alicia face eviction for the second time this year(n)

programme.title: Angel and the Badman (1947)

Western, starring John Wayne, Bruce Cabot and Gail Russell(n)

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes: Terror By Night (1946)
programme.desc: The sleuth and his faithful sidekick Dr Watson hunt for the killer of an aristocrat who hired them to keep a priceless diamond out of the hands of thieves. Mystery, starring Basil Rathbone, Nigel Bruce, Alan Mowbray, Dennis Hoey and Mary Forbes(n)

programme.title: The Court-Martial of Billy Mitchell (1955)
programme.desc: In the 1920s, an American colonel accuses the war department of criminal negligence for denying the air force the funding it needs - but the powers-that-be still regard aircraft as an irrelevant part of the military, and respond by putting him on trial. Courtroom drama, starring Gary Cooper and Rod Steiger, with Ralph Bellamy and Elizabeth Montgomery(n)

programme.title: White Christmas (1954)
programme.desc: A pair of former soldiers start a new career as nightclub entertainers. They try to boost the popularity of their old commanding officer's struggling winter resort in Vermont by staging a series of extravagant shows, with a little help from their former army buddies and a talented singing sister act. Musical, starring Bing Crosby, Danny Kaye, Vera-Ellen, Dean Jagger, Sig Rumann, Mary Wickes, Anne Whitfield and Rosemary Clooney, featuring seasonal songs by Irving Berlin(n)

programme.title: Lemony Snicket's A Series of Unfortunate Events (2004)

Quickly realising he is up to no good, the children flee, moving from one prospective guardian to the next - but their wily relative is never far behind. Comedy adventure based on the children's books, starring Jim Carrey, Meryl Streep, Jude Law, Liam Aitken and Emily Browning, with Kara and Shelby Hoffman(n)

Action comedy based on a comic book, starring Colin Firth, Taron Egerton and Samuel L Jackson(n)

As he rises to take his place in the White House during the Civil War, he discovers the bloodsuckers are the real power behind the Confederacy. Fantasy thriller based on Seth Grahame-Smith's novel, starring Benjamin Walker and Dominic Cooper(n)

programme.title: Rudderless (2014)

Macy, starring Billy Crudup, Anton Yelchin and Felicity Huffman(n)

programme.title: Decision at Sundown (1957)

The man he blames now has total control of the town and has the sheriff at his beck and call. Undeterred, the drifter offers his enemy an ultimatum - he has until sundown to leave the town or face retribution for his crimes. Western, with Randolph Scott, John Carroll and Andrew Duggan(n)

Western, starring Robert Mitchum, Teresa Wright, Dean Jagger, Judith Anderson, Alan Hale and John Rodney(n)

In the process, the trail leads him to a secret organisation that helped Nazi SS officers escape justice. Thriller, based on Frederick Forsyth's novel, starring Jon Voight, Derek Jacobi and siblings Maria and Maximilian Schell(n)

Western, starring Yul Brynner, George Segal, Janice Rule and Pat Hingle(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Confident economics undergraduate David is next to host in Aberdeen, but will his student grub earn him 10 out of 10 with his guests? The 1920s Gatsby-themed dress code goes down a storm with almost everyone, on a night featuring food ending up on the floor, smoke alarms going off and some entertaining cocktail-making(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Oil and gas purchasing specialist Sylvian hosts the third dinner party in Aberdeen, hoping her attention to detail and an ambitious global themed menu will be enough to impress her guests. Philippines-born Sylvian's dishes are an East-meets-West affair, starting with Japanese gyoza, followed by a Sumatran rendang and ending with a very British sticky toffee pudding. But dessert turns out to be sticky in more ways than one; cue tears before bedtime from the host(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Aberdeen, it's the turn of comedy promoter Naz to host. If Naz is to stand a chance of winning the competition, he must find a way to placate his guests, nearly all of whom he has managed to offend over the week. Naz is, by his own admission, not the greatest cook and things don't go according to plan and much of his food is burned and difficult to chew. However, revealing the inspiration behind her starter might be a step too far for her guests, especially after the revelations found inside her 'special' cupboard!(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics

Gordon is dismayed to discover unprotected meat and fish left next to mouldy pesto jars in the kitchen and plans to close down the eatery until radical changes are made(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped

programme.title: Food Unwrapped

Kate Quilton investigates what actually goes into sodium-replacement salts and examines whether salt really deserves its unhealthy reputation(n)

Her guests are 52-year-old vintage shop owner Jane, 28-year-old football coach Dan, 57-year-old art enthusiast Julie and 47-year-old pub landlord Gary. Gail gets the glitter, sparklers and hair extensions out, but whether her food will get glowing reviews ramins to be seen(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Goalkeeping coach Dan hopes to thrill his guests with a James Bond evening in Middlesbrough, including 007-inspired food and a Bond villain. however, Julie and Jane fail to 'bond' on the night, falling out over everything from who's been the best James Bond, to how to pronounce Roger Moore. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

Host Stephen Fry asks a range of fiendish questions on the topic of keys, with points being awarded for interesting answers as well as correct ones(n)

programme.title: Jon Richardson: Ultimate Worrier
programme.desc: Jon is joined by Joe Wilkinson, Lucy Beaumont and Kiri Pritchard-McLean to address worries to do with The Self. Has Joe fallen in with a bad crowd?(n)

programme.title: Comedians Giving Lectures
programme.desc: Stand-up comedians are given the titles of real lectures and invited to give their own version. Presented by Sara Pascoe. With Nish Kumar, Natasia Demetriou and Tom Allen(n)

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner presents the light-hearted panel show, which sees comedy actress Miranda Hart, TV and radio host Reggie Yates and Countryfile presenter John Craven competing to have their pet hates banished to the infamous room for ever. Their gripes include stuffed marrows, spitting, the hip-hop handshake and Miranda's breasts(n)

programme.title: The Russian Revolution in Colour
programme.desc: The conclusion of the two-part documentary charting the events following Lenin's rise to power, as the anticipated communist society was superseded by totalitarianism. After an attempt on his life, Lenin instituted a severe crackdown on political opponents and the bourgeoisie that inspired opposition from a number of factions and led to the assassination of the royal family(n)

programme.title: The Normans
programme.desc: Professor Robert Bartlett travels from Britain to Jerusalem via Sicily, examining the expansion and unchecked ambition of the Norman empire, and how its impact still resonates in today's culture and politics. He discovers how, under the leadership of Scandinavian raider Rollo, these descendants of the Vikings first established themselves in northern France and became an unstoppable force of Christian knights and warriors(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson explores the history of the ancient kingdom on a walk from Melrose in the Scottish Borders to Holy Island off the coast of Northumberland. Over five days, he goes on the trail of one of Northumbria's greatest sons - Saint Cuthbert. Through the Tweed Valley and across the Cheviot Hills, it's a journey through a murky history laden with myth. However, by investigating the communities, power bases and landscape of seventh-century Northumbria, Tony aims to understand the pagan land in which Cuthbert spread his simple brand of Celtic Christianity(n)

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: A Scottish soldier who walked 200 miles through the Libyan desert without water or supplies recalls Ronald Moore, the leader of his party(n)

programme.title: WW2 - Battles for Europe
programme.desc: Examining the work of the American forces who launched a huge offensive to blast a gap in the German lines and break through to liberate France(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The extension of the Panama Canal to accommodate the world's largest ships, which drew inspiration from the work of 17th-century engineer Hugues Cosnier(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Abandoned projects of the space age, including the HARP gun in Barbados and the mountainside Barcroft Observatory in California(n)

programme.title: The Normans
programme.desc: Professor Robert Bartlett examines the impact of the conquest of Britain and Ireland, and learns how William the Conqueror built scores of castles and cathedrals to impose his control and intimidate the population. The ruler also commissioned the Domesday Book to survey the wealth of the land and raise revenue, and set in motion the linguistic and cultural transformation of England. The Normans blended well in Scotland, but faced fierce opposition in Wales, and promoted divisions in Ireland which some claim still exist to this day(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson travels along the north Norfolk coast through one of the least developed spots in Britain, finding out how the area maintained its rural, remote identity despite the dynamic industrial changes of the Victorian age. From the arrival of Bertie, Prince of Wales at Sandringham House to the opening of Cromer Pier, Tony's four-day walk sees him learn about key periods in the county's history. He explores prime shooting estates, purpose-built seaside resorts, salt marshes and disused railway lines as he discovers how powerful landowners were able to control development and even shape the railways to suit their needs(n)

programme.desc: A profile of Dudley Mason, who was awarded the George Cross after the tanker he was commanding came under attack during Operation Pedestal in August 1942 to relieve Malta(n)

Guest starring Harold Perrineau (Lost, Oz) and Edward Furlong (Terminator 2, American History X)(n)

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Defence attorney Derek Powell's fiancee is killed during a robbery on his yacht, giving the CSIs reason to believe that his chequered past has come back to haunt him. However, just as the team is about to close in on the killer, questions about Delko's citizenship bring the case to an abrupt halt. Guest starring Sean 'P Diddy' Combs, with David Caruso, Emily Procter and Adam Rodriguez(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green are called in to identify the charred remains of a woman caught in an apartment block fire, which was started deliberately. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach(n)

programme.title: Law & Order

programme.title: Law & Order

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.title: The Coronation of Queen Elizabeth II
programme.desc: Documentary charting the historic events of June 2, 1953, when the Queen became the 39th sovereign to be crowned at Westminster Abbey. What happened behind the scenes is explored through diaries, letters and government papers, as well as rare film footage and photographs, and there are also interviews with historians and experts on royal ceremonies. The programme reveals that Her Majesty's apparent calmness during the Coronation contrasted with organisers' last-minute nerves as the BBC mounted its most ambitious television broadcast to date, amid the concerns of prime minister Winston Churchill(n)

programme.title: The Ivy
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes as eminent restaurant The Ivy undergoes its first major renovation in 25 years. The programme follows director Fernando Peire and executive chef Gary Lee as they oversee the London institution's re-launch. Features interviews with clientele, including Calvin Klein, Dame Joan Collins, Maggi Hambling, Ronald Harwood, Tracey Emin and Julian Fellowes(n)

programme.title: Spooks

programme.title: Spooks

programme.desc: With electronic cigarettes increasing in popularity, presenter Michael Mosley considers whether they are safe to use. programme.desc: Julia Bradbury and Matt Allwright profile the everyday heroes who battle the challenges that arise with each passing season. programme.title: Traders: Millions by the Minute
programme.desc: The concluding programme meets a new breed of trader - the British men and women who play the financial markets online from their kitchens, sofas and bedrooms. Mother-of-three Jane squeezes currency trading in-between the school run and her work as a nurse, and is hoping to raise capital by selling her 10 Bengal kittens. Justyn and Akil are both convinced that trading will be their ticket to a lavish lifestyle, while Rene gave up a successful career as an antiques dealer for a new existence as a trader, and is undeterred despite seven years of struggling to break even. Saskia Reeves narrates(n)

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner(n)
